content: Bill Sutton: Janey, come here. I need to talk to you. Janey Warwick: Bill Sutton, what's on your mind? Bill Sutton: You know how things have been around here... The way your mother used to treat me, then she up and left us. I didn't handle it very well. I drank, I stopped working, I let things fall apart. But that's all behind me now. I'm sober, I'm working hard, and I want you to know... I want to be a better father to you, Janey. I'm not perfect, but I'm trying to change. Janey Warwick: That's... That's really great, Dad. I'm happy to see you doing better. But... How do you know you're changing, deep down? Can a person really change, or are they always the same person underneath? Bill Sutton: Well, I've been thinking about that a lot. I know I've got a long way to go, but I believe that people can change.

Bill Sutton: I just hope I can prove it to you, Janey. And to myself. It's not easy, you know. But I want to be the father you deserve. Janey Warwick: I appreciate that, Dad. I really do. And I believe you can do it. Just keep trying, and we'll see how things go. Now, I think I'm going to get some rest. Bill Sutton: Alright, Janey. Sleep well. And if you need anything, just let me know. Janey Warwick: I will, Dad. Good night. Bill Sutton: Good night, Janey.
